issues.detail: Inline style tags cannot be cached. Moving to external CSS speeds up repeat visits.
issues.label: 9 inline style blocks
issues.check: View page source. Count the style tags. More than 3-4 is unwieldy.
issues.siteHint: Slower repeat visits, harder code maintenance.

issues.detail: Consider consolidating or deferring non-critical scripts.
issues.label: 25 external scripts
issues.check: Same check - DevTools Network tab, filter by JS.
issues.siteHint: Marginal speed improvement possible.

issues.detail: Without analytics, you cannot see how visitors find you or what they do on your site.
issues.label: No analytics tracking
issues.check: Check the page source for references to gtag, analytics.js, or fbq.
issues.siteHint: You are flying blind - no data on what pages work or where traffic comes from.

issues.detail: SSL cert renews on Jun 10 15:20:22 2026 GMT. Mark your calendar.
issues.label: SSL certificate expires in 39 days
issues.check: Check the lock icon in the address bar.
issues.siteHint: Just a reminder ÔÇö easy to forget until it is too late.

issues.detail: CSP helps prevent XSS attacks by controlling which resources can load.
issues.label: Missing Content Security Policy
issues.check: Check DevTools Console for CSP-related warnings.
issues.siteHint: A basic CSP blocks most common script injection attacks.

issues.detail: Without this, your site could be embedded in an iframe on another site (clickjacking risk).
issues.label: Missing X-Frame-Options header
issues.check: Check if the site has X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN in response headers.
issues.siteHint: Without this, others could embed your site on malicious pages.

issues.detail: The footer credits "Slider Revolution 6" as the developer, yet the underlying platform is WordPress. This suggests a pre-built theme rather than a custom build. The "developer" is essentially reselling a stock template.
issues.label: Website credited to "Slider Revolution 6" but runs on WordPress
issues.check: Scroll to the bottom of each page. Do you see "Designed by Slider Revolution 6"? They likely installed a WordPress theme.
issues.siteHint: Your current "web developer" delivered a WordPress theme, not a custom site. DS Studios can build something genuinely custom.

positives.detail: HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) is set

observations.label: Gift cards buried below reservations — zero visibility
observations.detail: There is a gift card/voucher page (/gift-cards) and a Toast eGift iframe on the reservations page, but neither is linked from the navigation (HOME, FOOD & DRINK, LIVE MUSIC, GALLERY, RESERVATIONS — no GIFT CARDS). The word 'gift' appears nowhere on the homepage or the menu page. A customer browsing the site has no way to know gift cards exist unless they dig into the bottom of the reservations page.
observations.suggestion: Add 'Gift Cards' or 'Vouchers' as a top-level nav item. Put a Gift Cards link in the footer on every page. Better yet, add a short 'Give the gift of JJ O'Malley's' section on the homepage above the fold with a direct call-to-action.
